D1 Optics L2 Understand Atom

Phase Shifter

slide a wave horizontally and watch its phase rotate around the unit circle

Shifting a sine wave by φ is equivalent to rotating its phasor by φ around the unit circle — the same operation underlies every interferometer.

Slide φ from 0° to 360°. The dark wave shifts horizontally; the crimson phasor on the right rotates counter-clockwise around the unit circle.
